beats.text: The first seal was broken the moment you entered. They are not physical seals — they are {knowledge_noun} seals. Every {discovery_verb} {knowledge_noun} peels back another layer. The vault is opening around you, not ahead of you.
Second seal. The {guardian_noun} was the mechanism. You were the key. The {material} around you shifts — the architecture of the vault adjusting to your unauthorized presence.
Three seals remain. The {atmosphere} thickens. Whatever is stored in the deepest chamber has been waiting a very long time. It senses the outer seals have broken. It is no longer entirely still.

The vault was not sealed to keep people out. It was sealed to keep something in. You are now deep enough to understand the difference. The {danger_noun} here is not the defenses — it is what you are letting loose.

The last seal. On the other side: {knowledge_plural} that were judged too consequential for the surface world. The {guardian_noun} who holds the final lock has never failed. You will be the first to test that record.

The vault is open. The final {knowledge_plural}, {discovery_verb} from their {container_noun} of {material}. The seal-makers are dust. Their judgment has been overruled. Consider carefully what you do with that authority.
The vault reseals. Your attempt is recorded in {material} somewhere in the lower chambers — another failed entry in a long list of failures. The {danger_noun} endures. The knowledge remains locked.

branches.text: The vault feeds on weakness. Every seal that held was held by something that bled.
Your relics resonate with the vault's {material}. They were made here, or made from things that were. The vault knows its own.
A seal tightens. The vault is not passive. It watches for hesitation.
